results.title: ccshare: multiplayer claude code
results.content:
I built ccshare, a tool for using terminal-based coding agents collaboratively with up to five people in the same live session. The idea came from trying to pair program with tools like Claude Code and Codex. Screen sharing lets another person watch, but only one person can interact with the agent. Passing commands back and forth or copying prompts between machines quickly becomes awkward. veryone sees the same terminal and the same agent conversation, and everyone can type. It works with Claude Code, Codex, and other interactive terminal-based tools. For people on the same network, ccshare can discover the session locally. There is also an option to connect directly to the host. Some use cases I had in mind: Pair programming with an AI coding agent Debugging together during a call [...] Subscribe
